executive_summary: The global system exhibits moderate instability characterised by acute energy volatility in the Middle East and escalating supply chain constraints. While physical fuel reserves remain secure, the significant divergence between Taiwan media narrative and actual physical events indicates high susceptibility to information warfare. This structural friction is exacerbated by persistent wealth inequality and speculative positioning within the technology sector.
correlations: The high variance in Middle East energy fluctuations directly compounds global supply chain stress through increased shipping risk premiums and transport cost uncertainty.
